As a member of the Orchidaceae family, Raphione pallens is classified as an epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants for support. The exact classification of Raphione pallens may be further specified within the subfamily Vanilloideae or the tribe Vanilleae. intro: Raphione pallens, also known as the Pale Raphione, is a species of flowering plant in the family Raphionaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis, high blood pressure, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Raphione pallens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic benefits. Raphione pallens is commonly used in herbal medicine to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for external use.
